mappings.rationale: Directly addresses A (the organization of work), its social dimensions, and C.1 (effects of rationalization on workers).
Examines B (application of scientific methods to managerial functions) including Frederick Taylor's scientific management and modern operations research.
Addresses B.2 (systems engineering) and B.3 (systems-design techniques) including organizational design and information technology infrastructure.
Treats B.2 (systems engineering) and the utilization of scientific and technological knowledge in designing complex production systems.
Traces the transformation from cottage industry to factory production, encompassing A.4 (16th-18th century production changes), A.5 (mass production), and A.6 (mechanization of labour).
Directly relevant to A.6 (machines as replacements for labour) and C (relation between man and machine in industrial production).
Addresses how intelligent systems automate decision-making and production tasks, relevant to A.6 (machines as replacements for labour).
Explores systematic approaches to improving production processes, relevant to B.1 (operations research) and continuous improvement methodologies.
Situates changes in production techniques (A.4) and mass production (A.5) within the broader capitalist economic framework and its logic of accumulation.
Directly listed in B.3.b as a tool for systems-design: optimization theory and game theory.
Addresses A (organization of work) and C.1 (psychological and social dimensions of mass production and automation on workers).
Explores decision-making and organizational direction-setting, relevant to B (application of scientific methods to managerial functions).
Traces the computing technology that underpins operations research and systems-design tools, relevant to B.3.
Illuminates A.1 (organization of work in the prehistoric world) including early division of labour and craft specialization.
Illustrates A.2 (organization of work in the ancient world), from slave labour and artisan workshops to large-scale construction projects like the Parthenon.
